title: narcissus minor var. parviflorus
intro: Narcissus minor var. parviflorus, also known as the small-flowered dwarf narcissus, is a plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and slender foliage. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include containing alkaloids such as galantamine, scopolamine, and homocholine, which have been used to treat a variety of health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus minor var. parviflorus include its potential use in treating neurological disorders, such as Alzheimer's disease, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its flowers and leaves, which are dried and used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions.
